Image created with GeminiThis blog series shares my thought process behind each NYT Connections puzzle I craft.
As a longtime fan of the game, I began making my own puzzles to reverse-engineer these brain-teasers and stretch my creativity. I’ve also noticed a parallel with solving business problems: both require analyzing a set of data and spotting patterns and relationships that aren’t immediately obvious.
Warning: the below contains answers for Bye Jill by Uyi Uwadiae on Connections Plus
🟨 Yellow: This category was from ‘Scat’ in Purple. One of my simplest connections. Also fits with ‘Chatter.’
🟩 Green: An homage to the Red and White Queens in Wonderland. Wines was the natural connection I made between the two.
🟦 Blue: Given that I had to find one more term for Purple and figured Humpty Dumpty being an egg would be a potential route (since ‘Share’ might be too obvious) I looked up words with a letter + egg and found ‘Yegg.’ Given its definition, I crafted this category around theft. ‘Appropriate’ stands out as not being as obvious a connection among these.
🟪 Purple: Seeing a commercial for the movie ‘Malice’ made me notice how the word is M + Alice, so I wondered if there were other Through the Looking Glass characters that a letter + their names/being formed a word. This might have also been around the time that the Sabrina Carpenter’s Alice in Wonderland movie was announced.
Find more of my Connections on Connections Plus!
You can play NYT Connections daily online or through the NYT Games app, available in the Apple App Store and Google Play.
